FLAG FOR OWNER: the registry codes the place as Marulanda (DANE 17446) and the date as 1963-05-05, while every press source places it in vereda La Italia, municipio Victoria, on 1963-08-05 — likely a coding/date-transposition artifact for the same event; verify before display.

I found zero primary (CNMH/Comisión de la Verdad/JEP/Rutas del Conflicto/Verdad Abierta/court) or peer-reviewed scholarship sources describing this exact event, so the >=3 primary/scholarship floor is not met; not forcing a draft. The event is unambiguously identifiable: the Colombian Army's VII Brigade ground assault (Operación Centauro 90) on 6 Nov 1990 against the FARC-EP Central General Staff / Eastern Bloc command (EMBO) in rural Uribe (La Uribe), Meta — matching the coded place, date, combat (acciones bélicas) type, Ejército Nacional perpetrator, and the 35 figure. No published source unambiguously describes an ACCIONES BÉLICAS event in El Doncello, Caquetá on 2002-06-03 with ~34 victims attributed to the Fuerza Aérea. The event is real and well documented: on 25 March 2000 roughly 300 FARC-EP combatants of fronts 34 and 57 overran the urban center of Vigía del Fuerte (Antioquia), killing ~20-21 police and ~8-9 civilians including mayor Pastor Damián Perea and two children, using gas-cylinder bombs and machetes. The >=3 primary/scholarship floor is therefore not verifiably met, so no draft. Reported toll (~29 total) differs from the registry's Total_Victimas_Caso of 33 — present 33 as the registry figure if drafted later.

The 3-source primary/scholarship floor is not met, so no draft.

Extensive searching across Rutas del Conflicto, JEP Caso 10, Comisión de la Verdad final report, HRW World Report 2003, Wikipedia violence chronologies (1990-2002 and 2002-2018), CNMH/OMC portal, Semana's victims chronology, El Colombiano archives, and multiple targeted searches returned no published source independently corroborating an acción bélica in Valencia, Córdoba on 2002-08-05 attributed to FARC with 29 victims. The event type (armed combat between FARC and security forces, not a massacre) is rarely documented in publicly accessible secondary sources at this level of specificity for 2002; the CNMH SIEVCAC registry entry is the sole identified source and does not meet the three-source primary/scholarship floor required for a draft.

The June 25, 2005 armed action in Puerto Asís, Putumayo corresponds to the major FARC assault on the Teteyé military base (Fronts 48 and 32, ~400 combatants), which multiple tertiary sources confirm at that date and location. However, the CNMH codes the perpetrator as "AGENTE DEL ESTADO / EJÉRCITO NACIONAL - FUERZA AÉREA" — meaning the registry records the Air Force/Army as the acting party responsible for the 29 victims, likely referencing a counter-air-strike within the engagement. No primary or scholarly source independently corroborates this specific framing (Air Force action, 29 victims); all available coverage attributes the attack to FARC and reports 19–25 military deaths. The ≥3 primary/scholarship floor is not met.

FATAL (Check 2): The narrative misrepresents its own cited primary source on the central disputed claim. It states Medicina Legal found 'no evidence of short-range gunfire on ANY of the examined bodies' / 'sin indicios de disparos a corta distancia en NINGUNO de los cuerpos'. The cited El Colombiano article (director Carlos Valdés) actually says FOUR of the 27 bodies had gunshot wounds ('solo en cuatro de los cuerpos se presentaban lesiones por proyectil'); what was absent was close-range firing RESIDUE, not gunshot wounds. In a narrative whose explicit purpose is to adjudicate the FARC 'tiros de gracia' allegation, erasing the four gunshot-wound bodies materially distorts the source. This alone fails the confirm bar. | Check 1 PASSES: spot-checked primary URLs all describe this exact event (Guapi/San Agustín, May 21-22 2015, Frente 29). Extensive searching across CNMH microsites, Justice and Peace court records (BCB/Frente Sur Andaquíes proceedings), Comisión de la Verdad, Rutas del Conflicto, open Colombian data portals, academic PDFs on paramilitary activity in Caquetá, and general press archives found no published source that unambiguously identifies this specific kidnapping event (29 victims, San José del Fragua, 2002-05-28, alleged paramilitary perpetrator). The event is documented in CNMH SIEVCAC but has not been corroborated by any independently verifiable published primary, scholarly, or tertiary source retrieved. The required floor of three primary or scholarly confirming sources is not met.

Two contemporaneous archival documents in the Univalle Digital Library confirm a massacre in Rovira, Tolima in 1959 attributed to bandoleros (including the group of Teófilo Rojas Varón, alias 'Chispas'), and two regional press sources (El Nuevo Día, Qhubo Ibagué) corroborate a specific attack on the vereda La Palmita on May 9, 1959 killing 25 campesinos. However, only 2 primary sources could be confirmed — short of the 3 required — and no peer-reviewed scholarship or CNMH/Comisión de la Verdad/Rutas del Conflicto page was found that unambiguously covers this exact event. There is also a material discrepancy: press sources cite May 9, 1959 and 25 victims, while the CNMH registry records May 19, 1959 and 28 victims, possibly reflecting a multi-day event or a data-entry difference.

The only plausibly related press source found is an El Tiempo article published February 22, 2002, reporting combat in El Placer/El Queremal, Dagua (FARC Frente 30 vs. paramilitaries, ~45 dead: 27 FARC + 18 paramilitary) beginning around February 20 — date and location are consistent but the victim count (45 vs. 28 coded) and the inter-armed-group nature of the clash leave the match uncertain. No primary sources (CNMH microsite, JEP, Comisión de la Verdad, Rutas del Conflicto, Fiscalía, court rulings) were found that unambiguously identify IdCaso 17340 with 28 victims on 2002-02-20 in Dagua. The ≥3 primary/scholarship threshold is not met.

Exhaustive search across Colombian conflict archives (CINEP/Noche y Niebla, Rutas del Conflicto, Comisión de la Verdad, CIDH, HRW, Amnesty International, UCDP, El Tiempo archivo, Semana, El Espectador, Wikipedia chronologies) found no published source that unambiguously describes an armed action (acción bélica) in Dagua, Valle del Cauca on or around September 7, 1999 with 28 victims attributed to the Ejército Nacional. The only documented Dagua combat events in open sources are the March 2001 Toma del Cerro Tokio (FARC vs. Navy infantry) and a February 2002 FARC-paramilitary clash in El Queremal — neither matching date, perpetrator, or victim count. The SIEVCAC registry entry is the sole record found; no independent corroboration reached the ≥3 primary/scholarship floor required.

Extensive searching in Spanish and English across CNMH Observatory pages, Rutas del Conflicto, JEP case documentation, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, Wikipedia chronologies, and press archives found no published source that unambiguously describes a November 10, 1992 combat action (acción bélica) in Valle del Guamuez, Putumayo with 28 victims attributed to the Ejército Nacional. The only well-documented Putumayo event close in date is the Toma de Churuyaco (November 7, 1992, Orito municipality, perpetrator FARC, 26 police killed), which is a different municipality, date, and perpetrator. The JEP Caso 03 Putumayo sub-case involves the Batallón de Infantería Héroes del Valle del Guamuez, but that unit operated 1998–2012, postdating this record by six years. No primary, scholarship, or tertiary source independently corroborating this specific event was found.

Extensive searching found no source that unambiguously confirms a forced disappearance event in Barrancabermeja, Santander on May 6, 1998 with 28 victims. The only well-documented mass forced disappearance event in that municipality and month is the "Masacre del 16 de mayo de 1998" (AUSAC paramilitaries, 7 killed + 25 disappeared = 32 victims), which is thoroughly documented by Rutas del Conflicto, CNMH, CEJIL, CAJAR, Amnesty International (AMR23/037/1998), the IACHR (Case 12.434), and the Inter-American Court (Cañas Cano y otros vs. Colombia). The three-variable mismatch — date (May 6 vs. May 16), victim count (28 vs. 32), and attributed perpetrator (GRUPO ARMADO NO IDENTIFICADO vs. AUSAC paramilitaries) — prevents safe identification of IdCaso 236683 with that event. It may represent a separate, less-documented incident or a different disaggregation within the same operation; no confirming primary or secondary source was found for the specific coded record.

Exhaustive web searches in Spanish and English across CNMH portals, Rutas del Conflicto, Wikipedia violence annexes, Banco de la República, DeJusticia, and Tolima-specific academic and historical sources yielded no published source that unambiguously identifies this specific event — an atentado terrorista in Alvarado, Tolima on 4 May 1958 with 27 victims. The event dates to the final days of La Violencia, a period with very sparse event-level documentation outside the CNMH's own OMC database; the minimum threshold of 3 primary or scholarship sources cannot be met.
